note: Frozen wire vectors for the messenger WebSocket protocol (docs/24, phase 8b). cmd/messenger (Go) is the server; the widget (kilden-sdk-js) and the panel inbox are the clients. docs/24 is the prose contract — this file is the executable one. Change both in the same PR.

conventions.envelope: Flat objects discriminated by `type`. No nested `payload`: fewer bytes on the wire (the widget ships under a byte budget) and a natural discriminated union in TypeScript.
conventions.unknown_fields: Receivers IGNORE unknown top-level fields (forward compatibility: an older widget must survive a newer server). They never echo them back.
conventions.unknown_type: An unknown `type` is ignored with a debug warning, never an error frame — same stance as the in-app renderer registry (docs/25).
conventions.timestamps: UTC, exactly YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.mmmZ (millisecond precision, literal Z). Same strictness as kilden-sdk-spec.
conventions.uuids: Canonical lowercase hyphenated. Message ids are UUID v7 GENERATED BY THE CLIENT: the primary key is the client's uuid, so dedup on reconnect is ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (docs/24).
conventions.equality: Tests compare PARSED structures, not bytes: key order is not part of the contract (Go, PHP and TS order differently). What is pinned: key names, value types, presence and optionality.
conventions.body: Plain text, 4096 bytes max (KILDEN_MESSENGER_MAX_BODY). Never HTML: every renderer escapes on display.

entities.note: These shapes cross a repo boundary: cmd/messenger emits them inside `msg.new`/`history.page`, and the panel serializes the SAME rows as Inertia props for the inbox. If they drift, the inbox renders one way on WS append and another way after a reload. Both sides pin this vector.

message.body: Hi! Does the free plan include session replay?

message_from_agent.body: It does — 30 days of retention on Free.

entities.conversation_visitor_note: What a visitor may see about its own conversation. Deliberately NOT the agent projection: assignment and the agent's read state are internal. The agent projection is served as Inertia props by the panel, never over the visitor socket.

visitor_to_server.name: auth_anonymous_first_time
visitor_to_server.note: No conversation_secret yet: the visitor has never opened a conversation. It may create one, and may read NOTHING (docs/11: no anchor, no reads).

visitor_to_server.name: auth_anonymous_returning
visitor_to_server.note: Possession of the secret IS the authentication for anonymous visitors (docs/24). The distinct_id alone never is: it travels in analytics.

visitor_to_server.name: auth_identified
visitor_to_server.note: Identified visitors need a valid JWT to read ANYTHING, in every project mode (docs/11 — the messenger always requires verification for reads).

visitor_to_server.name: msg_send_new_conversation
visitor_to_server.note: No conversation_id: the server creates the conversation and answers conv.created before msg.ack.

frame.body: Hi! Does the free plan include session replay?

visitor_to_server.name: msg_send_replayed_after_reconnect
visitor_to_server.note: Byte-identical replay of a queued message after a reconnect. The server MUST answer msg.ack again and MUST NOT create a second row (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on the client uuid). This vector is the dedup guarantee of the goal's definition of done.

visitor_to_server.name: history_older_page
visitor_to_server.note: `before` is a message id: page backwards from it (exclusive).

visitor_to_server.name: typing
visitor_to_server.note: Ephemeral: never persisted, never replayed on history.

server_to_visitor.name: conv_created_anonymous
server_to_visitor.note: `secret` is emitted ONLY for anonymous conversations and ONLY once, at creation. The widget persists it next to the anonymous_id; the server keeps only its SHA-256. It never appears in auth.ok, history or any later frame.

server_to_visitor.name: conv_created_identified
server_to_visitor.note: No secret: the JWT is the anchor (docs/11). Emitting one would create a second, weaker credential for an identity that already has a strong one.

server_to_visitor.name: msg_ack
server_to_visitor.note: The ack carries conversation_id so it is self-contained (the client correlates without depending on frame order) and created_at so the optimistic bubble gets the server's ordering. It carries NO separate `id`: the primary key IS the client's uuid, so an `id` field would always echo `uuid` back — one contract, one value.

message.body: It does — 30 days of retention on Free.

server_to_visitor.name: history_page
server_to_visitor.note: Oldest first. has_more drives the widget's infinite scroll upwards.

messages.body: Hi! Does the free plan include session replay?
It does — 30 days of retention on Free.

frame.message: identity token is invalid or expired

server_to_visitor.name: error_rate_limited
server_to_visitor.note: Non-terminal: the connection stays open. retry_after_ms is advisory for the widget's composer.

agent_to_server.name: msg_send
agent_to_server.note: No auth frame on /agent: the ticket in the query string authenticated the handshake. conversation_id is always required — an agent never opens a conversation in 8b.

frame.body: It does — 30 days of retention on Free.

server_to_agent.name: msg_new
server_to_agent.note: An agent receives msg.new for EVERY conversation of its project — the inbox list and the open thread update from the same stream.

message.body: Hi! Does the free plan include session replay?

invalid_frames.note: Malformed on their face: a receiver refuses these by SHAPE, before any session state is consulted. Sequence rules live in `protocol_rules` — they are about when a frame arrives, not what it looks like.
invalid_frames.name: msg_send_body_empty
invalid_frames.reason: empty body: nothing to say, nothing to persist

invalid_frames.name: msg_send_uuid_not_v7
invalid_frames.reason: the primary key must be a UUID v7 (version nibble 4 here): a v4 breaks the time ordering the thread relies on

invalid_frames.name: msg_send_uuid_missing
invalid_frames.reason: without a client uuid there is no dedup key

invalid_frames.name: auth_missing_distinct_id
invalid_frames.reason: the distinct_id anchors the conversation to a person

invalid_frames.name: msg_send_conversation_id_not_uuid
invalid_frames.reason: conversation ids are canonical uuids; a lookup key that cannot be one is refused before it reaches Postgres

invalid_frames.name: typing_with_client_declared_sender
invalid_frames.reason: `sender` is the server's word about who is typing; a client does not get to assert it. Left open, a visitor sends sender:'agent' and any server that forwards the decoded frame — the obvious implementation — shows 'the agent is typing' in the inbox on demand. The field is outbound-only, which is why the same key is legitimate on typing_from_agent.

invalid_frames.name: agent_opens_conversation
invalid_frames.reason: an agent never creates a conversation in 8b, so a msg.send with no conversation_id is malformed ON THE AGENT SIDE and fine on the visitor's — which is why `side` exists here: the same bytes mean different things depending on which handshake they arrive through

protocol_rules.note: Well-formed frames refused because of WHEN they arrive, not what they look like. That is the whole distinction from invalid_frames: these MUST decode cleanly, and a receiver refuses them from session state. Exercised against the running server, not the codec — but a codec test does assert they decode, because a malformed vector here would make a server test pass for the wrong reason.
protocol_rules.name: first_frame_not_auth
protocol_rules.reason: on /ws the auth frame is first and mandatory; anything before it is refused with 4401

protocol_rules.name: visitor_reads_foreign_conversation
protocol_rules.reason: the conversation belongs to another person: 4401, never a silent empty page

4401.meaning: not authenticated for what was asked: missing/invalid/expired identity token, wrong conversation_secret, a frame before auth, or reading a conversation that is not yours.
4401.note: The server sends the error frame and then closes with the same numeric close code, so the client can tell 'get a fresh token' apart from a network drop.

4403.note: Checked AFTER the upgrade and reported as a close code on purpose: a browser cannot read the status of a rejected WebSocket handshake, so an HTTP 403 here would surface as an unexplained 'connection failed'. A close code reaches the developer's console. A SECRET write key is different — it is refused before the upgrade with a pedagogical HTTP 403, mirroring /decide.

4429.meaning: rate limit (KILDEN_MESSENGER_MSG_RATE) or too many open conversations for this person.

not_in_8b.note: Deliberately absent so nobody implements them from the doc's older prose.
not_in_8b.conv_close_reopen_assign_frames: Status and assignment are written by the panel through Inertia actions (docs/24 write matrix). The acting agent updates its own UI optimistically — a client-side concern, not a frame. Other agents see the change on their next visit; realtime propagation of status is a known TODO for 8c.
not_in_8b.internal_messages_endpoint: POST /internal/messages (the dispatcher's path into a conversation) belongs to the in-app campaign channel, 8c.
